engine.ladder_reasoning: No defined expertise sold and no portfolio of work; the site is a presence page for a physical computer shop — founder story, an Instagram photo feed, and a 'Find Us' block with address/phone/WhatsApp/hours ARE the decision (should I visit/contact this store).

goal.one_action: Get in touch with / visit the shop (contact via WhatsApp/phone or the linktr.ee hub)

structure.archetype_note: WordPress presence site; nav also lists an off-domain 'Contact' pointing to linktr.ee. Blog has 1 post, Threads page is nearly empty; sidebar (About This Site + Find Us) repeats site-wide.

unmapped.why_no_fit: Titled nav page with no unique body content beyond repeated sidebar widgets

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full, but the site is unusually thin/inconsistent (empty Threads page, 1 blog post, prices found in raw HTML from an inner product page not crawled) and the primary contact CTA is split between an off-domain linktr.ee redirect and the on-page WhatsApp/phone, making the true conversion mechanism ambiguous.

gap_findings: Primary CTA is a link-in-bio (linktr.ee) redirect that aggregates all contact channels off-domain — sits between 'redirect' and 'call-WhatsApp' and isn't cleanly one mechanism.

Live embedded Instagram social feed is mapped to gallery/portfolio, but a running social-media feed widget (auto-updating, not a curated portfolio) is arguably a distinct capability the closed vocab can't cleanly express.
